title: Floral Creamy Tunisian Chicken Tagine
description: A lazy Sunday dish featuring tender chicken, sweet raisins, and fragrant floral notes, all slow-cooked to perfection in a creamy sauce. This Whole30-friendly recipe uses sous-vide cooking for ease and convenience.
instructions: In a large bowl, whisk together ras el hanout, dried rose petals, and dried orange blossoms.
Add the chicken to the bowl and toss to coat evenly with the spice mixture.
Seal the chicken in a sous-vide bag with the chicken broth and cook for 2 hours.
After 2 hours, remove the chicken from the bag and set aside.
In a blender or food processor, combine coconut cream, raisins, parsley, lemon juice, salt, and black pepper. Blend until smooth.
Add the cooked chicken to the blender and stir to coat with the creamy sauce.
Serve the chicken hot, garnished with additional parsley and rose petals if desired.
Let the dish rest for 10 minutes before serving to allow the flavors to meld together.
Refrigerate or freeze any leftovers for up to 3 days.
